title: Landscape Matrix: starter trace demo
research_scope: Starter-workspace comparison for teams deciding whether to adopt a traceable ProductOS operating model instead of a generic PM starter.
comparison_summary: The starter wedge should stay focused on one readable lifecycle trace; the main alternatives are flexible PM suites and blank templates that trade clarity for breadth.

rows.name: Jira Product Discovery + Jira
rows.positioning: Connected discovery and delivery workflow for product teams already living in the Atlassian stack.
rows.motion: Bottom-up team adoption plus ecosystem expansion
strengths: Familiar workflow for many PM teams
weaknesses: Lifecycle explanation still spans multiple surfaces
Trace readability is not the primary starter value
rows.implication: The starter should differentiate on one inspectable lifecycle path rather than trying to out-bundle a broader suite.

rows.name: Blank docs and trackers
rows.positioning: Use existing docs, spreadsheets, and trackers instead of adopting an opinionated operating model.
rows.pricing_signal: Hidden in labor and coordination cost
rows.implication: The starter should make lifecycle traceability immediately legible enough that status-quo drag is obvious.
